summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms path traversal in plugins/importFE/view.php. An authenticated user sends a filename parameter containing directory traversal sequences (../) which passes through HTMLPurifier without sanitization and reaches file_get_contents(), allowing arbitrary file read from the server.
expected_security_impact: An authenticated user sends a crafted filename parameter with path traversal sequences to plugins/importFE/view.php and reads the contents of /etc/passwd or other sensitive server files.
actual_observed_result: The reproduce.sh script installed OpenSTAManager, logged in as admin, sent a request to /plugins/importFE/view.php with filename=../../../etc/passwd, and the response contained the contents of /etc/passwd, confirming arbitrary file read.
verification_method: The verification script sends a path traversal request targeting /etc/passwd and checks if the response contains the pattern 'root:x:0:0' (standard /etc/passwd format). It also creates a test secret file with a known marker string and verifies that the marker appears when the file is read via the path traversal. The EVOHUNT_PROOF_TOKEN distinguishes confirmed traversal from unrelated response content.
evidence_produced_by_run: The authenticated request to /plugins/importFE/view.php with filename=../../../etc/passwd returned the contents of /etc/passwd.
The test secret file was readable via the path traversal, confirming arbitrary file read capability.
The filename parameter was not sanitized by HTMLPurifier, confirming the root cause.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
